title: Glencore notes ruling by the Chilean Second Environmental Tribunal regarding Collahuasi
gist: Glencore says a 15 May 2026 Chilean Second Environmental Tribunal ruling purports to set aside the environmental authorisation for the Collahuasi copper mine's capacity-expansion and desalination project, but the company does not currently expect any immediate impact on production given existing alternative water sources, while Collahuasi seeks clarification on the ruling's scope.

insights.claim: Chile's Second Environmental Tribunal issued a 15 May 2026 ruling that purports to set aside the Environmental Authorisation (RCA) issued in 2021 for the Collahuasi copper mine's Infrastructure Development and Production Capacity Improvement project, which includes a now nearly-complete water desalination plant.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Adds regulatory overhang to a major copper expansion; watch for clarification on whether the desalination/capacity project can proceed.
insights.quote: Glencore notes the 15 May ruling in Chile by the Second Environmental Tribunal which purports to set aside the Environmental Authorisation (RCA) issued by the Environmental Assessment Service (SEA) to the independently managed Collahuasi copper mine in 2021
insights.locator: Glencore notes the 15 May ruling in Chile

insights.claim: Glencore states it does not currently expect any immediate impact on Collahuasi production, citing existing alternative water sources for the mine.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Near-term copper output appears protected, limiting immediate supply-disruption risk despite the legal setback.
insights.quote: subject to the existing alternative water sources for Collahuasi, Glencore does not currently expect any immediate impact on production
insights.locator: Based on the information currently available

insights.claim: The tribunal's ruling is limited to two specific aspects relating to analysis of effects on a local community and on the marine environment, and Collahuasi is seeking full clarification from the Tribunal and the SEA on its scope and required re-examinations.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: The narrow scope (community and marine-environment analysis) suggests a remediable procedural issue rather than a full project block.
insights.quote: its ruling is limited to two specific aspects relating to analysis on the effects on a local community and on the marine environment
insights.locator: Glencore understands from the Tribunal's own press release

insights.claim: Collahuasi maintains the project's environmental permitting followed local regulations, with the RCA obtained in December 2021 after a SEA-led assessment including indigenous consultation, subsequently confirmed by the Ministers Committee in August 2023.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: A documented multi-stage approval history strengthens the case for the permit to be upheld or re-validated on appeal.
insights.quote: The RCA was obtained in December 2021, following a rigorous assessment process led by the SEA, including the indigenous consultation defined by the competent authority. This was subsequently confirmed by the Ministers Committee in August 2023.
insights.locator: Collahuasi has confirmed that the environmental permitting process
